skill.name: slack-gif-creator
skill.description: Knowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Provides constraints, validation tools, and animation concepts. Use when users request animated GIFs for Slack like "make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack."
skill.summary: Knowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack with PIL-based drawing, easing functions, and file size optimization.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: All 55 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate GIF creation toolkit using PIL for programmatic frame generation with easing functions. No cryptographic code, no network requests, no shell execution, and no system reconnaissance. The code examples in SKILL.md showing Python code with backticks are standard markdown formatting for code blocks.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: C2 Keywords
dangerous_patterns.description: Variable 'c2' in easing.py is a mathematical constant for back easing functions, not C2 (Command & Control) malware indicator.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: c2 = c1 * 1.525 is a standard easing math formula, not a security threat

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner incorrectly flagged import statements and standard library usage as cryptography. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Files import math, numpy, and pathlib - none are cryptographic

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Backticks in SKILL.md are markdown code formatting, not shell execution. The code examples show Python PIL drawing code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Documentation shows Python code blocks with proper import statements

content.value_statement: Create animated GIFs optimized for Slack using Python PIL. Generate custom animations with easing functions and automatic file size optimization for emoji and message contexts.
actual_capabilities: Create animated GIFs programmatically using PIL drawing primitives (circles, polygons, text)
Apply easing functions (bounce, elastic, back, linear) for smooth motion
Optimize GIF file size for Slack emoji (128x128) or message (480x480) contexts
Validate GIF dimensions and file size against Slack requirements
Generate gradient backgrounds and composite visual elements into frames
Apply animation concepts (shake, pulse, bounce, spin, fade, slide, zoom, explode)
limitations: Does not provide pre-made animation templates or graphics library
Requires Python PIL, numpy, and imageio dependencies
Cannot render emoji fonts (unreliable across platforms)
No support for video input or video-to-GIF conversion

use_cases.title: Slack Emoji Creation
use_cases.description: Create custom animated emoji for Slack workspace reactions and expressions
use_cases.target_user: Slack workspace administrators and team members wanting custom emoji

use_cases.title: Visual Response Generation
use_cases.description: Generate animated visual responses for messages and announcements
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and marketers using Slack for communication

use_cases.title: Technical Documentation
use_cases.description: Create animated diagrams for technical documentation and presentations
use_cases.target_user: Developers and technical writers explaining processes visually

prompt_templates.title: Basic Emoji GIF
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 128x128 animated GIF for Slack emoji showing a [SHAPE] that [ANIMATION]. Use 12 frames at 10 FPS with vibrant colors.

prompt_templates.title: Bouncing Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a bouncing animation GIF with [OBJECT] that lands at the bottom using bounce_out easing. Target 128x128 emoji size.

prompt_templates.title: Pulsing Logo
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pulsing [ICON] animation in [COLOR] that scales between 0.8 and 1.2. Save as 480x480 message GIF.

prompt_templates.title: Particle Explosion
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an explosion effect with particles radiating outward from center. Optimize for emoji size with minimal file size.

output_examples.input: Create a bouncing ball emoji GIF
output_examples.output: A 128x128 animated GIF showing a colored ball falling with bounce easing, 12 frames, ~20KB file size
A 480x480 pink heart that scales up and down smoothly, 15 frames at 12 FPS
A 128x128 golden star rotating 360 degrees, optimized for emoji with 48 colors

best_practices: Use optimize_for_emoji=True for small file sizes under 128KB
Apply easing functions instead of linear motion for polished animations
Keep animations under 3 seconds for emoji context to maintain engagement
Use vibrant contrasting colors with thick outlines (width=2+) for visibility
anti_patterns: Avoid using linear easing for all animations - use bounce, elastic, or back for natural motion
Do not exceed 256 colors for emoji - stay at 48 or fewer for optimal file size
Avoid generating frames without deduplication - use remove_duplicates=True to reduce size

faq.question: What dimensions should I use for Slack emoji?
faq.answer: Use 128x128 pixels for emoji GIFs. For message GIFs, 480x480 is recommended.

faq.question: How do I reduce GIF file size?
faq.answer: Use fewer frames (lower FPS), fewer colors (48 or less), smaller dimensions, or enable remove_duplicates=True.

faq.question: Can I use this to animate uploaded images?
faq.answer: Yes, use PIL to load uploaded images and apply transformations, or use them as color/style inspiration.

faq.question: What animation effects are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports shake, pulse, bounce, spin, fade, slide, zoom, and particle explode effects.

faq.question: How do I validate my GIF meets Slack requirements?
faq.answer: Use validate_gif() or is_slack_ready() from core.validators to check dimensions and file size.

faq.question: What dependencies are required?
faq.answer: Install with: pip install pillow imageio numpy
